title: More Nonsense Limerick 90
content:
There was an old man of Blackheath,
    Whose head was adorned with a wreath
    Of lobsters and spice, 
    Pickled onions and mice,
    That uncommon old man of Blackheath.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Edward Lear delivers a powerful performance in "More Nonsense Limerick 90"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
